patch.value:
## Context

File: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:35 — TELEMETRY_MAX_QUEUE_ENTRY_ATTEMPTS = 15.
File: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:1152 — entries with attempts >= 15 are pruned during flush.
File: src/cli/commands/health.ts:1163-1187 — warning generation; no check on max_attempts distribution.
File: src/cli/commands/telemetry.ts:232 — buildTelemetryStatsBuckets already tracks max_attempts per bucket.

Live observation: pm telemetry stats shows max_attempts: 8 for several commands in the current queue. If the endpoint stays unreachable, entries will silently hit 15 and be purged.

## Acceptance criteria

- Health telemetry check warns `telemetry_queue_high_retries:<count>` when any queue entry has attempts >= TELEMETRY_MAX_QUEUE_ENTRY_ATTEMPTS - 3 (i.e. 12+).
- Remediation registry entry: `telemetry_queue_high_retries` → `pm telemetry flush` + summary about endpoint reachability.
- The warning is advisory (does not flip ok:false per existing advisory policy).
- Unit test for the threshold boundary.

## Evidence

src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:35 — constant 15.
src/cli/commands/health.ts:1170-1187 — warning block, no retry-exhaustion check.
Live queue: max_attempts=8 observed (pm telemetry stats output 2026-06-07).
Telemetry queue max_attempts backlog health warning: surface items near retry exhaustion in pm health

The telemetry queue prunes entries when attempts >= TELEMETRY_MAX_QUEUE_ENTRY_ATTEMPTS (15). The current health check only warns when `active_failure || neverFlushed || highWater` (health.ts:1177). Events that are incrementally failing and approaching exhaustion (e.g. 10–14 attempts) silently disappear from the queue on the next flush cycle with no operator/agent signal. This is a loss of observability signal without any warning.
Advisory warning emitted when any queued event has attempts >= 12; remediation_map present in health --json; does not flip ok:false; unit test covers boundary.

- Health telemetry check warns `telemetry_queue_high_retries:<count>` when any queue entry has attempts >= TELEMETRY_MAX_QUEUE_ENTRY_ATTEMPTS - 3 (i.e. 12+).
- Remediation registry entry: `telemetry_queue_high_retries` → `pm telemetry flush` + summary about endpoint reachability.
- The warning is advisory (does not flip ok:false per existing advisory policy).
- Unit test for the threshold boundary.
